items.title: Bitcoin analysts predict $300,000–$500,000 price in 2029. The math says no
items.description: Analysts predict a rally to $300,000 or more by 2029. But key data suggests the era of moonshots may be over.

items.title: Meta's Chief Data Officer Says Agentic Commerce is the "Next Tier of Business"
items.description: Alex Schultz says stablecoins are assumed inside Meta. The harder problem is getting the rest of the world there.

items.title: U.S. government digital dollar set to be banned tonight under housing law's CBDC limit
items.description: Despite President Donald Trump's refusal to sign Congress' bipartisan housing bill, it's set to go into effect at midnight, and its temporary CBDC ban along with it.

items.title: Hyundai becomes first major South Korean company to introduce internal stablecoin transfers
items.description: The initiative builds on a broader shift by companies exploring stablecoins to move money between international operations more efficiently.

items.title: OKX, MetaMask, Matter Labs back dispute resolution court for AI agents
items.description: The Genlayer Foundation is leading the 27-firm consortium that makes AI-based payments, escrow and dispute resolution interoperable.

items.title: Circle soars after securing U.S. trust bank approval in crypto expansion
items.description: The approval adds to a growing list of crypto firms seeking federal banking licenses as the industry moves into the regulated financial system.

items.title: Japan's 'invest locally' plan likely to spur demand for assets like bitcoin, gold
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 10, 2026

items.title: Crypto defies equity weakness as altcoin optimism builds into the weekend
items.description: The price of bitcoin hit $64,400, retesting a level it failed to penetrate on Monday, with a break higher opening the path toward the June 15 peak of $67,250.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Robinhood says its AI agent feature will ‘soon’ be assisting crypto traders
items.description: More than 70,000 agentic accounts have already been created by Robinhood equities and options traders since late May, when Robinhood launched a beta version of the product.

items.title: DOJ moves to dismiss charges against alleged $722M BitClub fraudster: Report
items.description: Matthew Goettsche was set to face trial in October for conspiracy to commit wire fraud and selling unregistered securities.

items.title: Kraken to overhaul app with AI investing assistant
items.description: The redesigned app will use AI to recommend trades and tailor investing tools around users' financial goals as Kraken expands into broader financial services.

items.title: Senate Democrats call for hearings into Trump’s ties to crypto amid CLARITY Act discussions
items.description: Five senators called for committee hearings to investigate US President Donald Trump’s policies potentially being influenced by crypto funding from United Arab Emirates-linked and other entities.

items.title: US CBDC ban to go into effect without Trump signoff on housing bill
items.description: Donald Trump confirmed on social media that he would not sign the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which includes a ban on a US CBDC until the end of 2030, setting the stage for the bill to become law on Saturday.

items.title: New Hampshire council votes down $100M Bitcoin bonds
items.description: State representative Keith Ammon said the New Hampshire executive council's vote against Bitcoin-backed bonds was “short-sighted,” urging the body to reconsider the measure.

items.title: Backpack joins race for 24/7 stock markets with tokenized equities
items.description: The launch comes as tokenized stocks become one of the fastest-growing corners of crypto, with exchanges and traditional finance firms racing to expand 24/7 trading.

items.title: SK Hynix rises 13% in Nasdaq debut. Chairman tells CNBC 'demand is enormous'
items.description: SK Hynix has soared to a trillion-dollar market cap by serving some of the biggest names in technology, including Nvidia and Apple.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI alleging trade secret theft, says scheme was 'at every level'
items.description: The two companies entered into a high-profile partnership in 2024 when ChatGPT was integrated into the iPhone's operating system.

items.title: Trump says he won't sign housing bill, which would become law automatically
items.description: Congress passed the housing bill in June with strong bipartisan support amid concerns of rising home prices and institutional investors affecting the market.

items.title: AstraZeneca's trial flop raises a bigger question: Is its pipeline premium becoming more vulnerable?
items.description: For years, AstraZeneca has commanded one of the richest valuations among large European pharma companies on the assumption that it consistently delivers results.

items.title: OpenAI power consolidates under co-founder Greg Brockman ahead of prospective IPO
items.description: With Fidji Simo's official departure from OpenAI due to a chronic medical issue, Greg Brockman's role has become clearer.

items.title: The AI race is shifting from bigger models to cheaper, smarter systems
items.description: Companies are starting to choose AI models by task, cost and control, not just leaderboard rank.

items.title: Trump admin eases export controls for UAE; Warren blasts 'corrupt' provision
items.description: The Commerce Department will favorably review exports involving MGX, which used a stablecoin linked to President Trump's family for a $2B Binance investment.

items.title: Stablecoin issuer Circle just got the greenlight to operate as a bank. The shares are up 5%
items.description: Stablecoin issuer Circle surged in premarket trading after the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency granted it approval to operate as a trust bank.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=75LHvsMyzIo! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Police release man arrested on suspicion of murder of Ann Widdecombe
items.description: Devon and Cornwall force says 26-year-old is no longer part of the investigation

items.title: Sadiq Khan lobbies for Ed Miliband to become chancellor
items.description: Energy secretary wins support from London mayor but faces opposition from some Labour MPs over his net zero policies

items.title: Europe’s slow electrification is a ‘major mistake’, warns IEA chief
items.description: Fatih Birol says EU should have moved faster to achieve energy independence after 2022 gas crisis

items.title: London rental affordability improves despite recent rise
items.description: The salary needed to afford a rental home in the capital has fallen by 17% in the past year

items.title: Prepare for a perilous summer in markets
items.description: Potential hotspots include a reformist new Fed chair, weak Japanese yen and high-stakes earnings season

items.title: Wealthy warned over HMRC’s use of AI in tax crackdown
items.description: Advisers put rich on alert after authority says digital tools brought in billions in tax last year

items.title: Inside the risky race through the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Vessels that dare to use route close to Oman’s coast face increased risk of Iranian missiles

items.title: Further increases to UK minimum wage would be risky, warns Low Pay Commission
items.description: Economy may not be able to absorb raising wage floor further towards median earnings, says advisory body

items.title: Times Journalists Subpoenaed as Trump Escalates Pressure on Media
items.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel testimony from reporters who wrote about the new Air Force One. The Times called the move a “brazen act.”

items.title: New York Times Accuses Federal Employment Agency of Retaliation
items.description: A May lawsuit by the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission claims the newspaper discriminated against a white male employee who did not get a promotion.

items.title: SK Hynix Stock Rises on First Day of US Trading
items.description: SK Hynix began trading at $170 a share, above its initial public offering price of $149, in the latest test of investor demand for A.I.-related companies.

items.title: Shippers Face Deepening Dilemma as U.S. and Iran Vie for Control of the Strait
items.description: Companies desperately want to get their ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz, but face mounting risks. “Things are becoming uglier by the minute,” one executive said.

items.title: Atlassian Illegally Fired Worker in Rare Legal Victory for Tech Worker
items.description: A federal labor law judge determined last week that the software maker Atlassian had illegally fired an employee who questioned company policy changes.

items.title: A.I. Has Rewarded Investors. It May Now Pose Their Greatest Risk.
items.description: Near the midpoint of the year, stocks and bonds both report good returns. But the global stock market has become highly concentrated, our columnist says.

items.title: How Volkswagen’s Troubles Were Made in China
items.description: The German auto giant’s push into China powered it for decades, but now the company faces fierce competition from Chinese automakers in markets around the world.

items.title: The Work of Helping A.I. Destroy Work
items.description: Start-ups are paying white-collar professionals to teach their jobs to artificial intelligence models. It’s a bonanza. It’s bleak. Where will it end?
